content: APPROVE with advisory findings. (1) MEDIUM: deleteSessionCascade as top-level function in session_providers.dart — consider moving to separate module as file approaches 1000 lines. (2) MEDIUM: Schema v2 index created via raw SQL in migration but not declared on table class — idx_sessions_start_time_desc invisible to drift schema verification and not created by m.createAll() for fresh installs. Recommend @TableIndex annotation. (3) MEDIUM: session_detail_screen.dart constructs DAOs directly via SessionDao(ref.read(databaseProvider)) instead of using sessionDaoProvider — inconsistent with all other screens. (4) LOW: resumeSession read-then-update is non-atomic — document the assumption. (5) LOW: session_providers.dart at 956 lines — infrastructure providers could move to separate file. (6) LOW: Settings Clear All duplicates cascade pattern inline instead of using deleteSessionCascade equivalent.

APPROVE with changes. (1) MEDIUM: migration_v2_test.dart does not exercise actual onUpgrade code path — creates fresh v2 DB instead. Need real v1-to-v2 migration test. (2) MEDIUM: deleteSessionCascade top-level function has no direct test — only underlying DAO methods tested individually. (3) MEDIUM: followUpCount restoration test uses dynamic assertion (assistantCount-1) instead of pinned expected value — tautological test. (4) LOW: No widget test for Load older entries button appearance and tap behavior. (5) LOW: Empty guard has only 2 tests — missing re-entrancy guard test (endSession called twice concurrently). (6) LOW: No test for resuming an already-active (never-ended) session.

REVISE. (1) HIGH: N+1 query pattern — each _SessionCardWithCount fires independent getMessageCount query. 50 sessions = 50 DB round-trips at render time. Replace with single aggregating query returning Map<sessionId, count>. (2) MEDIUM: resumeSession read-then-update is non-atomic — use raw SQL customUpdate with resume_count + 1 expression for single round-trip. (3) MEDIUM: watchSessionsPaginated with growing LIMIT re-fetches all rows on every emission — document scalability ceiling or add cap. (4) LOW: Cascade delete without transaction — wrap in db.transaction for atomicity.

REVISE. (1) MEDIUM: deleteSessionCascade and discardSession lack transaction boundary — partial failure orphans data. Wrap in db.transaction(). (2) MEDIUM: discardSession clears state before DB deletion — if app killed between state clear and DB delete, orphaned session survives restart. Reverse order: DB first, then state. (3) LOW: Clear All does not check for active session — could corrupt in-progress session state. Add guard. (4) LOW: Resume guard susceptible to rapid double-tap race — add isResuming boolean guard. (5) LOW: No audit trail for Clear All — consider minimal tombstone. (6) LOW: Cloud-synced sessions persist in Supabase after local delete — document for user awareness.

Anti-groupthink analysis. KEY FINDING: Resume followUpCount miscalculation — formula assistantCount-1 counts closing/bridging messages as follow-ups, causing premature session end on resume. Hidden assumptions: (A1) no compliance needs used to justify 3 separate decisions, (A2) app-level cascade safe because no concurrent writes — but fire-and-forget sync creates real race, (A3) conversationMessages list grows unbounded on resumed sessions, (A7) followUpCount formula does not distinguish message types. Pre-mortem scenarios: phantom Supabase sessions after local delete, cascade-sync race, resume followUpCount triggers immediate re-close, memory growth on multi-resume sessions. Recommends: wasAutoDiscardedProvider should be enum on SessionState instead of global flag, transaction wrapping for deletes, followUpCount accounting fix.

Phase 6 Review Synthesis — APPROVE WITH CHANGES. 5 specialists dispatched (architecture-consultant, qa-specialist, performance-analyst, security-specialist, independent-perspective). Blocking findings (4): (B1) Resume followUpCount miscalculation — closing/bridging messages counted as follow-ups, causing premature session end on resume. Must fix before merge. (B2) Cascade deletes lack transaction boundary — partial failure orphans data. Wrap in db.transaction(). (B3) N+1 message count queries — 50 cards = 50 DB round-trips. Replace with single aggregating query. (B4) Schema v2 index not declared on table class — invisible to drift schema verification, not created by m.createAll() for fresh installs. Advisory findings (10): (A1) deleteSessionCascade placement in session_providers.dart. (A2) session_detail_screen constructs DAOs directly. (A3) migration test doesn't exercise real upgrade path. (A4) deleteSessionCascade has no direct test. (A5) followUpCount test uses dynamic assertion. (A6) discardSession state-before-persistence order. (A7) Clear All doesn't check for active session. (A8) Resume double-tap race guard. (A9) session_providers.dart at 956 lines. (A10) Pagination scalability documentation.
